anatomyBody: A great case prompt is not just longer. It makes the creative intent and engineering boundaries clear at the same time.
anatomyTitle: Why is this case worth sharing?
cardBody: A strong case study that combines visual direction, interaction design, code constraints, and acceptance criteria in one prompt.

cases.commerce: Commerce hero: define focus through product story
cases.dashboard: Data dashboard: convert metrics into narrative flow
cases.saas: SaaS pricing: turn objections into page structure

homeSections.casesBody: Each case includes an experience preview, full prompt, best-use scenarios, key ingredients, and acceptance criteria.
homeSections.casesTitle: Start from visual cases, not blank prompts.
homeSections.methodBody: We evaluate structure, mechanics, acceptance criteria, transferability, and output consistency.
homeSections.methodTitle: Use one standard to judge whether a prompt is excellent.

libraryTitle: Make excellent prompts browsable as an inspiration library.

methodPage.body: A strong case prompt is not simply longer. It clarifies intent, constrains the boundaries, explains the interaction mechanism, and defines acceptance standards.

principles.acceptanceText: Quality can be judged through copy, styling, behavior, and accessibility states.
principles.mechanicText: The core interaction or visual mechanic includes parameters, steps, and boundary conditions.
principles.structureText: Stack, assets, layout, states, responsiveness, and delivery standards are clearly separated.

methodPage.title: How to evaluate excellent prompts

notFound.title: Page not found

overview.body: PromptFolio helps teams preserve reusable prompt assets through structured detail pages, visual previews, copy-ready prompts, and quality breakdowns.
overview.pill: Curated for design, frontend, and AI product teams
overview.title: A professional knowledge base for excellent prompt cases.

sections.acceptanceText: Sizes, classes, copy, colors, and animation delays are measurable, which reduces subjective interpretation and rework.
sections.completeText: It separates stack, fonts, assets, layers, interaction, animation, and responsive rules so implementation can begin immediately.
sections.mechanicText: Instead of saying “follow the cursor,” it specifies the canvas mask, radius, gradient stops, and RAF smoothing parameters.

aurora.acceptance: The button uses CSS-only animation; hover feels subtly magnetic; focus is visible; mobile sizing never overflows.
aurora.bestFor: Brand homepage CTAs, campaign landing pages, sign-in primary actions, and premium product launch entrances.
aurora.body: Best for premium CTAs, launch pages, sign-in flows, or event entrances.
aurora.ingredients: Dark glass base, conic-gradient aurora border, internal shimmer, bloom shadow, hover lift, and reduced-motion fallback.
aurora.principle: This case separates the button into two layers: the outer wrapper uses a ::before pseudo-element to hold the rotating conic-gradient border, while the inner translucent dark span preserves label readability. Hover and focus only increase lift, saturation, and glow, so the material feels activated without changing the component structure.
aurora.prompt: Build a premium liquid aurora button using React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS. Use a rounded button wrapper with a ::before pseudo-element for an animated conic-gradient border that rotates through teal, blue, pink, and orange. Place a dark translucent glass inner span above the border, add a slow diagonal shimmer layer that travels across the label, and give the button a soft cyan bloom shadow. On hover and focus-visible, slightly lift and scale the button, increase saturation and glow, and show a clear focus outline. Keep sizing responsive, preserve strong contrast, and add a prefers-reduced-motion fallback that disables the conic spin and shimmer animations. Use CSS-only animations, no animation libraries.
aurora.title: Liquid Aurora Button

orbit.acceptance: Hovering a chip pauses the orbit; central copy updates with state; keyboard operation works; narrow screens keep clear hierarchy.
orbit.bestFor: AI product feature tours, SaaS homepage modules, interactive navigation, and product capability maps.
orbit.body: Best for AI tools, SaaS feature modules, or memorable creative navigation.
orbit.ingredients: Central product card, four orbiting feature chips, hover pause, selected highlight, fade/slide copy updates, and keyboard-accessible buttons.
orbit.principle: This case separates visual motion from information state: CSS owns the orbit and floating chip animation, while React state owns the active feature. Hovering, focusing, or clicking a chip only changes the active feature and pauses motion with animation-play-state, so the visual focus and central copy update together.
orbit.prompt: Create an interactive orbital feature selector in React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS. Define four feature objects with label, title, and body copy, then track the selected feature in React state. Render a central glassy product card and four orbiting feature chips as keyboard-accessible buttons. Hovering or focusing a chip should pause the orbit animation with animation-play-state, set it as the active feature, highlight it with a warm accent ring, and update the central title/body copy. Use lucide-react icons, visible focus states, smooth hover transitions, responsive spacing, and a prefers-reduced-motion fallback that disables orbital and chip float animations.
orbit.title: Orbital Feature Selector

glass.acceptance: The card must show convincing glass depth; pointer movement drives local spotlight and 3D tilt; hover brightens the edge and lifts controls; mobile screens do not crop content.
glass.bestFor: Premium subscription modals, AI tool upgrade prompts, fintech confirmations, and premium modal patterns in design systems.
glass.body: Best for frosted, luminous, high-end modal cards with pointer spotlight, 3D tilt, and sheen feedback.
glass.ingredients: Dark gradient stage, pointer-following spotlight, drifting color orbs, backdrop-blur glass panel, luminous border, diagonal sheen, 3D tilt, and lifted actions.
glass.principle: This case uses pointer position as the source of material feedback: onPointerMove converts cursor coordinates into CSS variables, radial-gradient layers use those variables to move the spotlight and refracted highlight, and rotateX/rotateY create a subtle 3D tilt. Decorative light layers disable pointer-events so the glass effect never blocks button interaction.
glass.prompt: Build a premium glassmorphism modal card using React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS. Create a dark cinematic stage with drifting blurred color orbs, a frosted glass modal panel, luminous border highlights, animated diagonal sheen, and clear primary/secondary actions. Add pointer-driven interaction: attach onPointerMove to the preview container, calculate the cursor position as percentages within the container, and write CSS variables --glass-x, --glass-y, --glass-rx, and --glass-ry. Use --glass-x and --glass-y in radial-gradient layers to create a cursor-following spotlight and refracted edge highlight. Use rotateX(var(--glass-rx)) rotateY(var(--glass-ry)) on the glass panel for subtle 3D tilt, resetting to center on pointer leave. On hover, brighten the border, increase the glow shadow, lift the icon/chips/buttons with translateZ or translateY, and keep all decorative light layers pointer-events: none so controls remain interactive. Include accessible buttons, strong contrast, responsive mobile behavior, and a prefers-reduced-motion fallback that disables floating, sheen, and orb animations.
glass.title: Premium Glass Modal Card

dogBrain.acceptance: The canvas must render a more detailed rotating canine brain: left/right cortex, frontal cortex, olfactory bulbs, hippocampus, brain stem, cerebellum, dense grooves, 44 neural nodes, and connection bundles must be visible; mobile layout stays readable and resources are cleaned up.
dogBrain.bestFor: Science education pages, pet-tech demos, veterinary health data visuals, and AI pet hardware launch pages.
dogBrain.body: Use Three.js to build a more detailed canine neural atlas with brain-region segmentation, olfactory tracts, hippocampus, cerebellum, brain stem, and a pulsing neural network.
dogBrain.ingredients: Three.js scene, PhysicalMaterial brain-region surfaces, left/right cortex and frontal pads, olfactory bulbs and tracts, hippocampus, cerebellum, brain stem, dense TubeGeometry grooves, 44 pulsing nodes, neural connection bundles, and canine skull plus ear contours.
dogBrain.principle: The refined model splits the brain into recognizable regions instead of relying on simple spheres: cortex, frontal pads, olfactory bulbs, hippocampus, cerebellum, and brain stem use distinct materials and proportions. Layered TubeGeometry creates cortical grooves, olfactory tracts, and neural bundles, while 44 emissive nodes pulse independently through requestAnimationFrame for a more explainable canine neural atlas.
dogBrain.prompt: Create a refined responsive Three.js case-study module in React + TypeScript for a stylized canine brain atlas. Build the model procedurally without external assets: separate left/right cortex lobes, frontal cortex pads, olfactory bulbs, hippocampus forms, cerebellum, and brain stem with distinct physical materials; add dense TubeGeometry cortical grooves across both hemispheres, cerebellar folds, olfactory tracts, neural connection bundles, and a subtle dog skull silhouette with ear contours. Scatter at least 40 emissive neural nodes across the lobes and animate independent pulses over time while the brain slowly rotates and floats. Use PerspectiveCamera, warm key light, green rim light, ambient fill, antialiasing, SRGB output, device-pixel-ratio clamping, ResizeObserver resizing, and complete cleanup of renderer, geometries, and materials on unmount. Overlay compact labels for Frontal cortex, Olfactory bulb, Hippocampus, and Cerebellum. The result should feel like a premium science-museum neural atlas, readable on desktop and mobile.
dogBrain.title: Dog Brain 3D Model Showcase

textTruncate.acceptance: The preview must show single-line ellipsis, multi-line clamp, middle truncation, gradient fade, wrapping tags, and expandable full copy; each strategy must define its use case, and long Chinese/English strings, filenames, and mobile widths must not break the layout.
textTruncate.bestFor: Content cards, file lists, product titles, notification centers, data tables, mobile feeds, and admin interfaces.
textTruncate.body: Compare multiple text truncation strategies so long titles, descriptions, filenames, and tags stay readable across containers.
textTruncate.ingredients: Single-line text-overflow, two-line line-clamp, middle truncation with suffix preservation, gradient fade, wrapping tags, expandable detail, title/aria-label full text, and responsive breakpoints.
textTruncate.principle: This case treats truncation as a content decision, not a universal crop. Titles use one-line ellipsis to keep lists aligned, descriptions use a two-line clamp for semantic density, filenames use middle truncation to preserve extensions, editorial copy can fade for a softer edge, tag groups wrap, and important content keeps an expandable route plus accessible full text.
textTruncate.prompt: Create a responsive copy truncation showcase in React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS. Build a compact UI lab that compares at least six text-overflow strategies: single-line ellipsis for titles, two-line line-clamp for descriptions, middle truncation that preserves a filename extension or identifier suffix, gradient fade for editorial copy, wrapping chips for tag groups, and an expandable details pattern for full text. Use realistic long Chinese and English copy, file names, product titles, and status labels. Keep the layout readable from mobile to desktop, use min-width: 0 wherever flex/grid truncation needs it, preserve full text through title or aria-label attributes, and explain which strategy is best for each content type. Add subtle visual hierarchy, clear focus states, and no JavaScript measurement unless absolutely necessary.
textTruncate.title: Copy Truncation Strategy Lab

showcaseBody: Here are five copy-ready prompt cases: one Three.js 3D showcase, one content UX case, two for silky CSS animation, and one for UI interaction. Each card includes a concrete visual treatment and the full prompt.
showcaseTitle: Excellent cases should show the experience, not just the text.
templateBody: The site can pair each prompt with the original text, teardown notes, quality tags, reusable templates, and visual previews.
templateTitle: Turn every case into a reusable prompt asset.
